channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The band playing when a Bangkok bar caught fire mourns its members among the 33 dead
item.description: Victims of this week's flash fire at a Bangkok music bar that took more than 30 lives included four of the six core members of the band playing when the blaze broke out.
item.content:encoded:
Victims of this week's flash fire at a Bangkok music bar that took more than 30 lives included four of the six core members of the band playing when the blaze broke out.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
item.description: The U.S. has fought three major wars in the past quarter-century, in Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan. None has gone as planned.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. has fought three major wars in the past quarter-century, in Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan. None has gone as planned.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Sen. Shaheen discusses new Russia sanctions bill
item.description: A conversation with Jeanne Shaheen, Democratic Senator from New Hampshire, about a bipartisan bill she put together with the late Senator Lindsey Graham that slaps new sanctions on Russia.
item.content:encoded: A conversation with Jeanne Shaheen, Democratic Senator from New Hampshire, about a bipartisan bill she put together with the late Senator Lindsey Graham that slaps new sanctions on Russia.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Harland-Dunaway

item.title: The crews stuck at sea as the Strait of Hormuz crisis grows
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is leaving thousands of crew members on commercial ships stranded. Arsenio Dominguez, head of the UN's International Maritime Organization, explains.
item.content:encoded: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is leaving thousands of crew members on commercial ships stranded. Arsenio Dominguez, head of the UN's International Maritime Organization, explains.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: China and Xi favored over U.S. and Trump in many nations: Survey
item.description: The change is driven by improved perceptions of China and declining views of the United States.
item.content:encoded:
The change is driven by improved perceptions of China and declining views of the United States.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hong Kong booksellers arrested for allegedly selling seditious books
item.description: Hong Kong was once known for its freedom of publication, but political changes have created a challenging environment for independent bookstores.
item.content:encoded:
Hong Kong was once known for its freedom of publication, but political changes have created a challenging environment for independent bookstores.

(Image credit: Kanis Leung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China's economy grows 4.3% in Q2, slowest since late 2022
item.description: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Old rivals, new battle: Argentina and England clash in World Cup Semifinal
item.description: Old rivals. New stakes. A World Cup final spot on the line. Argentina vs. England.
item.content:encoded:
Old rivals. New stakes. A World Cup final spot on the line. Argentina vs. England.

(Image credit: Stu Forster)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Alcoba

item.title: Greetings from Paris, where an art installation transformed the Pont Neuf into a rocky cave
item.description: The oldest bridge in Paris was draped in fabric last month to appear like a mountain cave, in an installation by the artist JR. Unlike a real cave, though, it didn't provide respite from a brutal heat wave.
item.content:encoded: The oldest bridge in Paris was draped in fabric last month to appear like a mountain cave, in an installation by the artist JR. Unlike a real cave, though, it didn't provide respite from a brutal heat wave.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: Acting AG Todd Blanche faces a key test. And, ICE pauses most traffic stops
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate for his confirmation hearing. And, Immigration and Customs Enforcement has paused most traffic stops after recent deadly shootings.
item.content:encoded:
Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate for his confirmation hearing. And, Immigration and Customs Enforcement has paused most traffic stops after recent deadly shootings.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton
